name: Faro de Gorliz and its bunkers — circular from Armintza
name_original: Faro de Gorliz y sus búnkeres — circular desde Armintza
region_intro: Hiking around Lemoiz offers a diverse landscape characterized by a transition from rural farmsteads and green meadows to rugged coastal cliffs. The region features spectacular coastal views where sheer cliffs meet small bays and pebble beaches. This duality provides varied scenery for hikers, from peaceful green expanses to dramatic seaside vistas, and is recognized as a Relevant Natural Space. Best hiking trails around Lemoiz The most popular hiking route is Faro de Gorliz and its bunkers — circular from Armintza, a 8.8 miles (14.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 25 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers coastal views and historical bunkers. Another top favourite among local hikers is Urizarmendi y Santa María de Lemoiz — circular desde Armintza, a moderate 5.6 miles (9.0 km) path. This trail ascends Urizarmendi hill, providing panoramic views before descending towards Armintza. Local hikers also love the GR 280 Uribe Trail, a 12.6 miles (20.3 km) trail leading through diverse Basque landscapes, often completed in about 5 hours 47 minutes. Hiking around Lemoiz is defined by coastal paths, rural farmsteads, and dramatic cliffs. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Lemoiz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 800 reviews. More than 6000 hikers have used komoot to explore Lemoiz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Lemoiz?
A: Lemoiz offers a diverse landscape, transitioning from traditional country farmsteads and verdant meadows to rugged coastal cliffs. You'll experience spectacular coastal views where sheer cliffs meet small bays and pebble beaches, alongside peaceful green expanses.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Lemoiz?
A: Yes, Lemoiz has trails suitable for beginners. An easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14997188" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Armintza Trail loop from Lemoiz&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 5.8 km (3.6 miles) long and takes around 1 hour 34 minutes to complete.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can visit near the hiking routes?
A: Lemoiz is close to several significant landmarks. You can visit the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/275684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint John of Gaztelugatxe&lt;/a&gt;, famous for its hermitage and 241 steps, or explore the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1516121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Butrón Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/476513" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorliz Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; also offers panoramic coastal views.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Lemoiz?
A: Yes, Lemoiz features several circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1328598979/urizarmendi-y-santa-maria-de-lemoiz-circular-desde-armintza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urizarmendi y Santa María de Lemoiz — circular desde Armintza&lt;/a&gt; trail offers panoramic views after ascending Urizarmendi hill. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10510129" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Billano Island – Gorliz Lighthouse loop from Lemoizko zentral nuklearra&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Lemoiz?
A: The hiking routes in Lemoiz are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 870 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse scenery, from dramatic coastal cliffs to peaceful rural paths, and the well-maintained trails.

Q: Can I find any challenging or difficult hikes in the Lemoiz area?
A: For those seeking a more challenging experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1327806828/gr-280-uribe-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 280 Uribe Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 20.3-kilometer (12.6 miles) route that leads through diverse Basque landscapes, including forests and coastal views, typically taking almost 6 hours to complete.

Q: Is Lemoiz a good place for birdwatching while hiking?
A: Yes, Lemoiz is recognized as a Relevant Natural Space and serves as a nesting ground for seabirds. Coastal trails, particularly those connecting Armintza to Sopela and passing through the Txipio wetlands, offer excellent opportunities for birdwatching.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Lemoiz?
A: Lemoiz offers enjoyable hiking throughout much of the year due to its coastal climate. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, while summer is ideal for coastal walks, though it can be busier. Winter hiking is also possible, but be prepared for cooler, potentially wetter conditions.

Q: Are there any coastal walks that offer views of lighthouses?
A: Absolutely. A popular coastal journey is from Armintza to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/476513" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorliz Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, which provides stunning 270° panoramic views from the summit of Mount Ermua. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/527262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matxitxako Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; is also situated on a prominent cape, ideal for spotting cetaceans and seabirds along coastal paths.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in Lemoiz?
A: There are 11 hiking routes available in Lemoiz, catering to various difficulty levels from easy to difficult. This includes 2 easy, 8 moderate, and 1 difficult trail.

Q: Can I find information on circular walking routes starting from nearby towns like Gorliz?
A: Yes, for additional circular walking routes in the wider area, particularly those starting from Gorliz, you can find more information on the &lt;a href="https://www.visitgorliz.eus/en/24-circular-walking-routes-from-gorliz/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Visit Gorliz website&lt;/a&gt;. This can provide further options for exploring the region.

Q: What kind of natural features define the Lemoiz hiking experience?
A: The Lemoiz hiking experience is defined by its dynamic natural features, including dramatic coastal cliffs, small secluded bays, and pebble beaches where the land meets the Cantabrian Sea. Inland, you'll find rolling green meadows and traditional farmsteads, creating a striking contrast.
tips: Armintza (or Arminza, in Spanish) is a small coastal town in the municipality of Lemóniz. It is located in an incredible setting, in a natural cove protected by the tip of Kauko and the rock of Gaztelu.

We will find the geodesic vertex on top of a small stone construction.
From this area we can see Fraile Island (also known as Billano Irla or Villano Island).

Built in 1990, this lighthouse holds the title of being the highest altitude lighthouse not only in Euskadi, but also in the Cantabrian Sea.
From this point, we will also have privileged views over the Bay of Plentzia.
Just below are some bunkers built in the post-war period.

Located at the end of this cape in the Bay of Plentzia you will find this defensive area that is part of the Bilbao Iron Belt.
They were built with slave hands during the postwar period.
From this modest summit of just 127 meters above sea level we will have views over the entire Plentzia bay, its rocky outcrops and its beach.
